title: Run State Serialization & Deserialization
serialization_flow: Caller → RunState: to_string() [optional wrapper over to_json()]
RunState → RunState: _serialize_approvals() → reads context._approvals → returns dict[tool_name → {approved, rejected, rejection_messages?, sticky_rejection_message?}]
RunState → RunState: _serialize_model_responses() → for each ModelResponse: {usage, output[], response_id, request_id}
RunState → RunState: _serialize_original_input() → normalises assistant messages (adds 'status':'completed', wraps string content)

RunState → RunState: _serialize_context_payload(context_serializer, strict_context) → tries Mapping > context_serializer > model_dump > dataclasses.asdict > {} + warning; returns (payload, context_meta)
RunState → RunState: _serialize_item(item) for each generated_item / session_item → {type, raw_item, agent, output?, source_agent?, target_agent?, tool_name?, tool_lookup_key?, tool_origin?, ...}
RunState → RunState: _serialize_current_step() → if NextStepInterruption: {type:'next_step_interruption', data:{interruptions:[...]}} via _serialize_tool_approval_interruption()

RunState → _serialize_pending_nested_agent_tool_runs: for each function_run peek_agent_tool_run_result() → if pending nested RunResult with interruptions → nested_state.to_json() embedded as entry['agent_run_state']

RunState → Caller: returns dict with $schemaVersion, current_turn, current_agent, original_input, model_responses, context, generated_items, session_items, current_step, last_processed_response, last_model_response, trace, sandbox?, tool_use_tracker, guardrail results, conversation_id, previous_response_id, reasoning_item_id_policy, generated_prompt_cache_key
deserialization_flow: Caller → RunState: from_string(initial_agent, state_string, context_override?, context_deserializer?, strict_context?) → json.loads() → from_json()
Caller → RunState: from_json(initial_agent, state_json, context_override?, context_deserializer?, strict_context?) → delegates to _build_run_state_from_json()
_build_run_state_from_json: 1. validate $schemaVersion against SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSIONS
_build_run_state_from_json: 2. _build_agent_map(initial_agent) + _build_agent_identity_map(initial_agent) via BFS over handoffs + agent-as-tool instances
_build_run_state_from_json: 3. resolve current_agent via _resolve_agent_from_data() (identity key > name lookup)

_build_run_state_from_json: 4. restore context: context_override (RunContextWrapper|Mapping) > context_deserializer(mapping) > plain Mapping restore; set usage; call context._rebuild_approvals(approvals_dict); restore tool_input
_build_run_state_from_json: 5. construct RunState(context, original_input, starting_agent, max_turns, conversation_id, previous_response_id)

_build_run_state_from_json: 6. state._model_responses = _deserialize_model_responses() → for each entry: deserialize usage + output items (ResponseOutputMessage, ResponseFunctionToolCall, ResponseComputerToolCall, ResponseReasoningItem, etc.)
_build_run_state_from_json: 7. state._generated_items = _deserialize_items(generated_items, agent_map)
_build_run_state_from_json: 8. state._last_processed_response = await _deserialize_processed_response(last_processed_response_data, current_agent, context, agent_map)
_deserialize_processed_response → current_agent.get_all_tools(context) → build maps: FunctionTool/ComputerTool/CustomTool/LocalShellTool/ShellTool/ApplyPatchTool/HostedMCPTool/Handoff
_deserialize_processed_response → _deserialize_action_groups(): for each action group (functions/computer_actions/etc.) call _deserialize_actions() with tool_map + call_parser + action_factory

_deserialize_processed_response → await _restore_pending_nested_agent_tool_runs(): for each function_entry with 'agent_run_state' → recursively await _build_run_state_from_json() → _SerializedAgentToolRunResult → record_agent_tool_run_result()
_deserialize_processed_response → deserialize mcp_approval_requests, interruptions via _deserialize_tool_approval_item()
_deserialize_processed_response → return ProcessedResponse(new_items, handoffs, functions, computer_actions, custom_tool_calls, shell_calls, apply_patch_calls, mcp_approval_requests, interruptions)
_build_run_state_from_json: 9. restore session_items (or merge from generated+processed if absent in payload)
_build_run_state_from_json: 10. restore current_step → NextStepInterruption from interruptions data via _deserialize_tool_approval_item()
_build_run_state_from_json: 11. restore guardrail results: input/output/tool_input/tool_output guardrail stubs
_build_run_state_from_json: 12. restore trace_state = TraceState.from_json(), sandbox, tool_use_tracker, reasoning_item_id_policy, generated_prompt_cache_key
_build_run_state_from_json → Caller: returns rebuilt RunState

processed_response_entry.interruptions: list[{type, raw_item, agent, tool_name?, tool_namespace?, tool_origin?, tool_lookup_key?, allow_bare_name_alias?}]

context_serialization_strategies: Mapping → direct dict copy (no deserializer needed)
strict_context=True → raises UserError if no safe path
important_notes: Schema versioning: CURRENT=1.9; all versions 1.0–1.9 supported. to_json() always emits 1.9. Older SDKs reject newer unsupported versions (fail-fast forward compat).

Nested Agent.as_tool() interruptions: serialized recursively inside last_processed_response.functions[n].agent_run_state; restored via _restore_pending_nested_agent_tool_runs() → record_agent_tool_run_result()
Deduplication: _merge_generated_items_with_processed() uses (id,type) and (call_id,type) sets to avoid duplicating items already in _generated_items
Approval persistence: always_approve/always_reject decisions survive serialization in context.approvals; per-call rejection_message also persisted (schema 1.6+)
to_string() is JSON string wrapper over to_json(); from_string() parses then delegates to from_json()
Agent identity for duplicate names tracked via _agent_tool_state_scope_id and agent_identity_keys_by_id map (identity != name stored as 'identity' field)
Trace, sandbox, guardrail results all serialized as optional fields; TraceState.from_json/to_json handles trace metadata including optional API key
